critical_incident_database.decision: Epictetus chose to make his own lameness an explicit and recurring pedagogical instrument in the Discourses — referring to himself as 'a poor old man, lame' rather than concealing or minimizing the disability — using the empirical fact of his impaired body to anchor doctrine that could otherwise be dismissed as easy for the able-bodied.
critical_incident_database.context: Throughout his Nicopolis decades, especially in the later books of the Discourses, Epictetus repeatedly referenced his own physical condition. The lameness, dating from the broken-leg incident under Epaphroditus (incident-001), was permanent and visible. He could have treated it as a private fact, mentioned only when unavoidable. Instead he made it a recurring datum: he used himself as the evidentiary case that the body could be among the externals without the doctrine collapsing.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the standard student-objection to the body-as-external doctrine was 'this is easy for someone whose body works.' Any able-bodied teacher faced this objection without an empirical answer. He noticed that he had, accidentally, the empirical answer in his own person: he was visibly lame, visibly aging, and visibly unaffected. He noticed that concealing the lameness would forfeit the empirical anchor and commit him to defending the doctrine on argument alone, which his own pedagogical method treated as inferior to demonstration.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the maximal pedagogical use of the empirical case his own body provided. A subordinate goal was modeling, for students, the appropriate relationship between a Stoic and his own body — using it as data, not as identity, neither concealing impairment from shame nor centering it as defining attribute.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected the conventional convalescent-hiding pattern, in which disability was minimized in public for status reasons. He rejected the alternative extreme of centering disability as identity, which would have inverted the doctrine he was teaching by making the body's impairment definitive of the self. He rejected the option of mentioning the lameness only as a confession of weakness — the wrong evaluative register. He rejected acquiring assistive technology (a litter, a slave-bearer) that would have removed the visible signal.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed his body as instrument-and-evidence rather than self. The lameness was data the cohort needed to see in order to absorb the doctrine; concealment would have been a kind of theft of evidence from his students. The framing was structurally identical to a doctor demonstrating an injection on himself to show students it was tolerable — the demonstration was instructive precisely because the instrument was real.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the lameness-references to function as standing rebuttals to the easy-for-the-able-bodied objection, both during his lifetime and in transmitted text. He expected his own death by physical decline to be similarly usable, on the same logic. He did not expect — but achieved — that the lame-old-man self-description would become one of the most-cited self-presentations in ancient philosophy.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A Stoic teacher of standard practice would, in the conventional case, have minimized references to his own physical condition, treated it as private, and defended the body-as-external doctrine on argument alone. The doctrine would have remained vulnerable to the easy-for-the-able-bodied objection because the teacher had no empirical answer, only the argumentative one. The lameness would have been a fact about the teacher's biography rather than a load-bearing instrument of the curriculum.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his pedagogical model preferred demonstration to argument when both were available, and the lameness gave him a demonstration that no other Stoic teacher of his stature could provide. Reference to his own body required overcoming the conventional shame-architecture around disability — a shame-architecture that, on his doctrine, was itself a category-error (treating an external as self-defining). The willingness to use his own body as instrument is therefore an instance of the doctrine successfully applied to its hardest test: the teacher's own lived condition.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The 'lame old man' self-presentation became one of the iconic features of the Discourses. It anchored the body-as-external doctrine empirically and survived through Origen's later confirmation of the broken-leg origin (incident-001). The self-presentation also functioned, post-transmission, as a stylistic signature: readers across two millennia have found the Discourses' first-person self-references unusually credible precisely because they include the lameness as ordinary datum. The pedagogical instrument outlived its instrumental purpose — it became, posthumously, evidentiary architecture.

critical_incident_database.decision: Epictetus chose to retain the diagnostic-uncomforting register when teaching even after Domitian's death (96 CE) and the restoration of philosophical freedom in Rome, declining to return to Rome and continuing the Nicopolis school under conditions where the political constraint that had originally driven him there no longer applied.
critical_incident_database.context: Domitian was assassinated in September 96 CE; his successor Nerva immediately reversed the philosopher-banishment edict. Trajan's accession in 98 CE consolidated the restoration. Several exiled philosophers returned to Rome. Epictetus was sufficiently visible in Rome before exile and in Nicopolis afterward that a return would have been straightforward and would have placed him in the prestige philosophical center of the empire. He chose not to return. The decision was sustained for the remaining ~37 years of his life.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the original political constraint had been replaced by an evaluative question: was Rome a better location for the school's actual function (transformation of students) than Nicopolis? He noticed that Nicopolis had developed a stable student-pipeline of exactly the right shape, and that disrupting it for the sake of Roman prestige would optimize the wrong variable. He noticed that the very impulse to return — now that the constraint was lifted — was itself diagnostic: it would mean the original 'maximize student-access' framing had been a rationalization for what was actually 'be in Rome if politically possible.'
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the continued operation of the school as it was. A subordinate goal was the demonstration to himself that the original Nicopolis-choice had been principled rather than constrained — a demonstration that required staying when the constraint was lifted. The non-return was therefore both an operational and a self-diagnostic act.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected return-to-Rome, which the conventional response would have been. He rejected splitting the school between locations, which would have diluted the teaching environment. He rejected periodic visits to Rome that would have provided social-prestige access without disturbing the school — a compromise that would have allowed the prestige-impulse to be partially indulged. He rejected the option of presenting the non-return as principled in public lectures, which would have converted the act into reputation-capital.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the question as a value-test: now that the constraint was gone, the only reason to return was the very reputation-and-prestige motive his doctrine identified as confused. The non-return was therefore the operational expression of the doctrine, and any reasoning toward return would necessarily be a rationalization. The framing was structurally identical to a sober alcoholic refusing the first drink that is now offered without coercion.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the non-return to be invisible to most of the philosophical community — a non-event. He expected the Nicopolis school to continue. He expected to die in Nicopolis. He did not expect — and would not have welcomed — recognition for the non-return as a principled act, because such recognition would have converted the act into the very reputation-stake the act was designed to refuse.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A philosopher of standard imperial practice would have returned to Rome at the first opportunity, re-established his school in the prestige location, leveraged the exile as biographical capital ('the philosopher who outlasted Domitian'), and acquired a larger student-body and higher-status alumni. The career trajectory would have followed conventional ambition. The doctrine would have remained articulate but would have lost the operational test of whether the prestige-impulse could be refused under conditions where refusal had no external constraint.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his framework treated the lifted-constraint case as the harder test, not the easier one. Refusing prestige under coercion is unambiguous; refusing it under permissive conditions tests whether the refusal was principled or only constrained. His commitment was to the harder test, even though it was less visible. The non-return is structurally one of the cleanest applications of his entire doctrine in his biography: the externals (Rome, prestige, larger student-body) were available and were declined precisely because their availability made the declination diagnostically meaningful.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Epictetus remained at Nicopolis until his death around 135 CE. The school operated continuously. Roman students continued to make the journey, including (per the Historia Augusta) the future emperor Hadrian. The non-return is rarely cited in ancient sources because its non-event quality made it invisible — exactly as Epictetus would have predicted. It is identifiable as a decision only by inference from the geographic continuity of the school across the Domitian-Nerva-Trajan-Hadrian transition.

critical_incident_database.decision: Epictetus chose to compare a philosophical school to a hospital rather than a theatre — and on that comparison, refused to please an entertainment-seeking auditor by altering his lecture-style toward performance.
critical_incident_database.context: Recorded as Discourses 3.23, the lecture addresses an unnamed auditor (possibly multiple) who had come expecting philosophical performance — eloquence, elevated rhetoric, edifying conclusions, and the affective satisfaction of having attended a performance. Epictetus' diagnostic style produced none of these reliably. The auditor, by an unspecified mechanism, communicated dissatisfaction or evaluation-by-performance criteria. Epictetus' response was not adjustment but the explicit reframing of the school as a hospital, in which discomfort is the necessary mode and 'leaving feeling well' is in fact a sign that nothing therapeutic occurred.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that auditors were applying performance-criteria (was the lecture eloquent? satisfying?) to an institution whose stated purpose was therapeutic. He noticed that any accommodation toward performance would convert the school into the very institution his teacher Musonius had refused to operate. He noticed that the cohort, observing the response, would learn the distinction between auditor-pleasing and student-treating.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the explicit rejection of performance-criteria as applicable to the school's function. A subordinate goal was the reframing of the auditor's dissatisfaction as data about the auditor's expectation-mismatch, not data about the school's quality. A further goal was preserving the diagnostic register against the steady cultural pressure to convert philosophy into performance.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected adjusting style toward auditor-satisfaction, which would have been the natural response to feedback. He rejected silently absorbing the dissatisfaction and continuing as before, which would have left the auditor's frame unchallenged for the cohort. He rejected denouncing the auditor without philosophical content, which would have substituted theatrics for diagnosis. He rejected the option of a partially-performative register on prestige occasions while maintaining the diagnostic style normally, which Seneca-style code-switching had demonstrated to corrupt.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the school as a medical institution and the lecture as a clinical procedure. The auditor's dissatisfaction was, on the frame, a category-error: complaining that surgery hurt was a misunderstanding of what surgery was. The framing was operationally specific — it instructed the cohort to evaluate the lecture by therapeutic effect, not by lecture-as-performance criteria.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the auditor to leave dissatisfied or to remain and reframe his expectations. He expected the hospital-frame to enter the cohort's repertoire as a transferable rebuttal to performance-criteria. He did not expect — but accepted — that some prestige-auditors would never return, reducing the school's cultural standing.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher of standard imperial-philosophical practice would have absorbed the audience-feedback and adjusted: more eloquence, more elevated argument, more satisfying conclusions, fewer uncomfortable diagnostic moments. The school would have grown in audience and prestige. The cohort would have absorbed, alongside the doctrine, the operational lesson that audience-pleasing was a legitimate criterion. The doctrine would have remained articulate while its operational character drifted into performance.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because the hospital-frame was, for him, not a metaphor but a description of the institutional function. A school that adjusted toward performance was simply a different institution; preserving the function required preserving the form, even at audience-cost. The hospital-comparison is also a precise example of his pattern of using a vivid, operationally specific framing to anchor a structural argument that abstract terms (educational seriousness, philosophical rigor) would have left vulnerable to drift.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Discourses 3.23 became one of the most-quoted passages of the Discourses, and the school-is-hospital comparison entered the philosophical-pedagogical tradition as a standard rebuttal to performance-criteria. The school's reputation was, for the rest of Epictetus' life, that of a place of diagnostic seriousness rather than entertainment. Auditors who came for performance generally left in the first weeks; the cohort that remained was self-selected on therapeutic-criteria, exactly as the framing required.

critical_incident_database.decision: Epictetus chose to deliver Discourses 1.10 — a sustained public attack on intellectuals who calibrate their work to advancement at Rome — to a Nicopolis audience that included Roman students who were themselves on advancement-tracks, accepting that the lecture would land as direct diagnosis on identifiable individuals in the room.
critical_incident_database.context: Discourses 1.10, titled 'To those who busy themselves about advancement at Rome,' is a recorded lecture in which Epictetus enumerates the daily corruptions of the courtier-philosopher: rising before dawn to attend salutations, calibrating one's reading to whoever is expected at dinner, treating philosophy as ornament for public-life ambition. His audience at Nicopolis was disproportionately composed of young Roman men whose families had sent them to acquire philosophical polish before returning to senatorial or equestrian careers. The lecture's diagnostic categories applied directly to them. He delivered it anyway.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the cohort contained the very type the lecture diagnosed, and that softening the lecture by audience composition would convert the school into the Roman-courtier-friendly institution his teacher Musonius had refused to operate. He noticed that the diagnostic only worked if it landed on individuals capable of recognizing themselves in it; an abstracted version would feel safe and leave the cohort unchanged.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was that specific students recognize themselves and either leave or change. A subordinate goal was the cohort's witness of the principle that the school's diagnostic register did not bend to audience-composition. He was deliberately accepting student-attrition as the cost of preserving diagnostic load.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected the abstract-philosophy-of-ambition framing that would have spared the present cohort. He rejected delivering the lecture only to a different cohort. He rejected the option of a softer follow-up after the lecture that would have reassured present students they were not the type. He rejected pre-screening attendance.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed audience-composition as a feature, not a bug, of the diagnostic. The students whose lives most resembled the diagnosed pattern were precisely the students for whom the diagnostic was useful. The lecture was clinical, not punitive — but it was clinical in the room.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected some attrition, some reactive defensiveness, and some genuine self-recognition. He expected the lecture to enter Arrian's transcripts (which it did) and to function as standing diagnostic literature for the same type across centuries.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher of standard imperial-philosophical practice would have delivered the same content in abstract terms, framed as warnings about a type of person not present in the room, allowing every student to identify the type with someone else. The students would have left philosophical-feeling and untransformed.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because he treated the in-room application as the operational test of whether the lecture was actually working. Abstract delivery was, on his analysis, indistinguishable from non-delivery: it provided doctrinal vocabulary for self-deception rather than diagnosis. The willingness to accept audience-loss as the cost of diagnostic-precision is a recurring pattern across his career.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Discourses 1.10 became one of the canonical Stoic critiques of court-philosophy and courtier-intellectuality. Some Roman students undoubtedly left. The students who remained absorbed the principle that the school's diagnostic was applied to whoever was present, including themselves, which functioned as a self-selection filter for the cohort that produced the textually-recorded student body.

critical_incident_database.decision: When a student asked how to behave with someone weeping in grief, Epictetus advised outward sympathetic expression — even joining in lament if needed to console — while strictly withholding inner assent to the proposition that the loss was actually evil.
critical_incident_database.context: Recorded as Encheiridion 16. The student's question concerned a concrete social situation: how does the Stoic act around a grieving friend without either cold doctrinal coldness or hypocritical performance. Epictetus' answer — that the outward behavior should be calibrated to the social need (sigh, even weep aloud, share words of grief) while the inner state retains the assent-discipline (do not believe the loss is evil; the friend's grief is itself the externals on his side) — resolves a tension other Stoic teachers handled less gracefully.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the student's question presupposed a binary — either the Stoic must internally grieve (collapsing the doctrine) or must refuse outward sympathy (becoming socially cold). He noticed the binary was a false dichotomy generated by conflating outward behavior with inner assent. He noticed that the social cost of refusing sympathy was real and that the doctrine did not require paying it.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was correcting the student's collapse of two distinct domains — outward-action and inner-assent — into one. A subordinate goal was preserving role-ethics (the friend role obligates social comfort) while preserving the assent-discipline (the inner state remains correctly calibrated).
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected the cold-Stoic answer (refuse outward sympathy as compromised performance). He rejected the consoling-Stoic answer (allow inner grief as appropriate to the role of friend). He rejected the silence-and-presence answer (be present, say nothing) as insufficient to the social demand. He rejected scripting fixed words because flexibility-by-context was structurally required.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the situation as a clean test of his architectural distinction between the two domains. Social action and inner assent operate on different criteria; an answer that respected both was not a compromise but the doctrine's correct application.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the student to absorb the architectural distinction and apply it across other domains (anger management, fear, polite speech under pressure). He expected the principle to enter the cohort's transferable inventory.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A doctrinally rigid Stoic would have advised refusing outward sympathy as inauthentic. A consolatory Stoic would have authorized inner grief as appropriate to friendship. Either answer would have failed by the doctrine's own criteria — the first by collapsing role-ethics, the second by collapsing the assent-discipline.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his version of Stoicism was architecturally precise about which domain the doctrine governed: the prohairesis, not the outward behavior. Outward behavior was a subject for role-ethics, which had its own criteria. The clean separation, once established, dissolved the apparent tension the student had registered.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Encheiridion 16 became one of the most cited and most replicated Stoic instructions across the early modern reception, including in Christian devotional reuse. The architectural distinction it operationalizes — outward calibration, inner stability — is the structure that allowed Stoicism to be lived under social conditions far removed from a Greek philosophical school.

critical_incident_database.decision: When a student arrived wishing to be a Cynic philosopher — adopting the dress, the staff, the begging — Epictetus chose to refuse encouragement and instead demanded the student understand the Cynic role as the highest possible duty, which the student was nowhere near prepared to assume.
critical_incident_database.context: Recorded as Discourses 3.22. A student announced his intention to become a Cynic. The Cynic identity in the imperial period had become diluted into a mendicant counter-cultural pose, with many self-styled Cynics adopting the dress and rhetoric without the underlying philosophical austerity. Epictetus' response inverts the student's expectation: rather than warning him against the Cynic life as too hard, he describes it as a divinely-authorized role of the highest order, then enumerates the qualifications the student lacks.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the student had selected the Cynic role for its identity-markers (visible difference, social signal, romantic counter-cultural status) rather than its philosophical content. He noticed that authorizing the choice would convert the student into another fake Cynic, of which the imperial period had a surplus. He noticed that refusing the choice required showing the student why the role was greater than the student understood, which was harder but more durable than dismissing the role itself.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was preventing the student from acquiring an identity-costume he had not earned. A subordinate goal was preserving the Cynic role's actual standing — refusing to let it be diluted by easy assumption. A further goal was teaching the cohort to evaluate role-claims on capacity rather than aesthetic preference.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected encouragement of the choice. He rejected dismissal of the Cynic role as low-status, which would have flattered the student into a different but equivalent error. He rejected silent toleration of the choice. He rejected redirecting the student to a different role entirely; the diagnostic was about the student's role-claim mechanism, not about which role.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the encounter as a role-ethics diagnostic: under what conditions is one entitled to claim a given role? The Cynic role was, on his analysis, a maximally-demanding role obligating the practitioner to be a 'scout for the human race' from God — a role whose qualifications most candidates would fail. The framing was structurally identical to evaluating a candidate for a difficult clinical specialty: the question was not 'do you want to' but 'are you capable.'
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the student to either back away (the high-probability outcome) or to undertake serious preparation. He expected the cohort to absorb the role-evaluation mechanism. He expected this transcript to function as a permanent rebuttal to easy Cynicism (which it did).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher in the consolatory register would have encouraged the student's enthusiasm. A teacher in the dismissive register would have mocked the Cynic role itself. Both would have failed to install the role-evaluation mechanism the encounter actually transmitted.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his role-ethics framework treated role-assignment as a serious matter requiring actual capability — the framework did not recognize identity-aesthetic as a basis for role-claim. His refusal to authorize the easy assumption was the framework operating cleanly on a high-stakes case.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Discourses 3.22 became the foundational text on Cynicism's actual philosophical content versus its costume, and shaped subsequent Stoic discussion of role-ethics. The student's identity is not preserved. The diagnostic mechanism — evaluate role-claims by capacity, not by aesthetic — entered the cohort's inventory and is identifiable in subsequent transcripts.

critical_incident_database.decision: Epictetus chose to structure the Nicopolis curriculum around three explicit topoi — the discipline of desire, the discipline of action, and the discipline of assent — in a fixed order of precedence, against the more conventional Stoic curricular structure that began with logic, physics, and ethics as parallel branches.
critical_incident_database.context: The three-topoi structure is identifiable across Discourses 1.4, 3.2, 3.12, and other lectures. It re-organized the Stoic curriculum from a doctrinal taxonomy (logic / physics / ethics) into a sequence of practical disciplines aimed at progressively transforming the student's operations: first regulating what one desires and avoids (so the prohairesis is calibrated to the dichotomy of control), then regulating what one does in social roles (so action serves duty rather than appetite), then regulating assent to impressions (the most sophisticated discipline, requiring the prior two). The curriculum was unconventional enough to require explicit defense within his own teaching.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the conventional curriculum produced students fluent in doctrinal taxonomy who were no more transformed than they had been at admission. He noticed that the operations the doctrine claimed to install were sequential — desire-discipline was a prerequisite for action-discipline, which was a prerequisite for assent-discipline — and that an order-respecting curriculum was therefore necessary, not optional. He noticed that the conventional curriculum's pretension of treating logic / physics / ethics as parallel was an artifact of doctrinal-systematicity goals that had no operational warrant.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the maximal probability of installation of the actual cognitive operations across a finite student-stay at the school. The curriculum was an instrument; its structure had to match the order in which the operations could actually be installed.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected the parallel-branches curriculum of Chrysippean Stoicism. He rejected an unstructured discipleship in which sequence emerged from student-need. He rejected explicit logic-first instruction (logic was a tool for the disciplines, not their content). He rejected explicit physics-first instruction (physics was background metaphysics, not a discipline of practice).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the school as a sequence of trainings rather than a corpus of doctrines. The curriculum was the order of trainings. The framing was structurally identical to a craft apprenticeship in which the apprentice cannot be taught the third stage before the second is mastered.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the curriculum to produce reliable installation of the disciplines in students who completed the full sequence, and reliable partial installation in students who left early. He expected critique from doctrinally-conventional Stoics (which the Discourses contain his responses to).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher of standard practice would have organized the curriculum around the conventional taxonomy and would have produced doctrinally-fluent but operationally-untrained students. The curriculum would have been defensible as systematic but would not have transmitted the operations that distinguished Stoicism from a doctrinal scheme.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his evaluation of the curriculum was operational rather than doctrinal. The order that produced installed operations was the correct order, regardless of whether it conformed to the doctrinal taxonomy. The willingness to depart from canonical structure for operational reasons is structurally identical to his other operational-over-conventional choices.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The three-topoi structure became Epictetus' identifiable contribution to Stoic pedagogy and was later treated by Marcus Aurelius (Meditations passim) as the operational architecture of self-examination. The curriculum's operational orientation is one of the structural reasons the Discourses produced students whose subsequent careers — Arrian, others — show identifiable transformation rather than mere doctrinal acquisition.

critical_incident_database.decision: Epictetus chose to refuse, in his own self-presentation and in his advice to students, the use of the title 'philosopher' — instructing students that one is not a philosopher by claim but by practice, and that the claim in advance of the practice was a category error.
critical_incident_database.context: Encheiridion 22, 33, and 46 record the instruction repeatedly: do not call yourself a philosopher, do not announce your principles, do not parade philosophical doctrines among the unphilosophical. Epictetus modeled this in his own self-references: he calls himself a teacher, an old lame man, a worker; he avoids the title. The reason given is operational: the title preceding the capacity invites both the student's and the audience's assumption that the capacity is present, which corrupts the student's own self-evaluation.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that students who began identifying as philosophers (using the title in their own self-references) developed a protective relationship to that identity that interfered with subsequent diagnostic work. He noticed that the title was an external whose value-system tracked reputation, not capability. He noticed that authorizing students to use the title was structurally identical to giving them a false credential.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was preserving the student's diagnostic accessibility. A subordinate goal was modeling, in his own self-presentation, the operation he was instructing.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected authorizing the title for advanced students. He rejected the claim that title-use was harmless. He rejected the consolatory teaching that took the title as the marker of completed work. He rejected the option of personally claiming the title while denying it to students.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed identity-claim as a leading-indicator of corruption: the student who began calling himself a philosopher had begun valuing the identity, which displaced the practice that the identity was supposed to mark. The framing was structurally identical to the no-fame-without-deeds principle in athletic training.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected students to absorb the practice-over-identity orientation and to detect its violation in themselves and others. He expected the principle to be operative pedagogical infrastructure, not an additional doctrinal claim.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher of standard imperial practice would have used the title, would have authorized advanced students to use it, and would have organized the school's reputation around the credential. The school would have been more institutionally durable and would have produced more credentialed graduates and fewer transformed ones.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his framework treated identity-claim as a precise diagnostic indicator of mis-located self. Allowing the title was indistinguishable from authorizing a category error. The refusal-of-title is therefore not a humility-pose but the framework operating on its own preconditions.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The principle entered the Stoic tradition as one of its identifying features and was preserved across centuries of reception. Modern readers consistently note the absence of self-promotional rhetoric in the Discourses; that absence is the operational consequence of the principle.

critical_incident_database.decision: When students and friends repeatedly urged Epictetus to retire or reduce his teaching load as his physical condition deteriorated in his late seventies and eighties, he chose to continue lecturing on the same daily schedule — accepting the increased physical cost as compatible with the role-duty he had taught for forty years.
critical_incident_database.context: By the late period (c. 125–135 CE), Epictetus' health was visibly failing — the lameness compounded by ordinary aging, with documented references in the Discourses to his frailty (e.g., Discourses 1.16.18, 3.5). The Nicopolis school's continued operation depended on his daily lectures; no successor had been designated, and the school had no institutional permanence beyond his presence. Friends and students had standing reasons to urge reduction; the conventional path for an aged philosopher was retirement to private writing or contemplation. He continued lecturing.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the urging-to-retire framing assumed teaching was a labor that could be set down, whereas his framework had always treated it as a role-duty whose cessation required justification rather than continuation. He noticed that the bodily cost was an external whose discomfort was not a sufficient reason on his doctrine. He noticed that retirement at the moment when retirement was urged would have demonstrated to the cohort that role-duty bent to bodily comfort, contradicting the demonstration the school had been operating for decades.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the consistency of his demonstration with his doctrine across the full life-arc, including the last and hardest segment. A subordinate goal was the school's continued operation. A further goal was the late-period demonstration that aging itself could be lived inside the framework — the empirical case that he had previously promised the cohort he would supply.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected retirement, which the conventional response would have been. He rejected reduced schedule, which would have been the moderate compromise. He rejected designating a successor and tapering, which would have institutionalized continuation but would have shifted his function from teacher to elder. He rejected writing his own treatises in old age, which would have substituted authorship for live teaching.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the late-period as the culminating test-case of the doctrine. The body's failure was the textbook external; continued role-performance was the textbook duty. Cessation would not have been a doctrinal compromise — Stoicism allowed retirement — but it would have been a missed demonstration. He chose the demonstration.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected to teach until the body's failure made teaching impossible. He expected the school to dissolve on his death, which it did. He did not expect institutional preservation; the school was instrument, not artifact.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A philosopher of standard practice would have retired in his seventies, written valedictory treatises, designated successors, and institutionalized the school. The school might have outlived him. The doctrine would have been preserved as written corpus. The demonstration that the doctrine could be lived through the entire arc, including frailty and decline, would not have been provided.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his framework treated demonstration as the operation of teaching itself. Retiring would have been a different doctrine — one that treated old-age cessation as appropriate. By his own role-ethics, the role of teacher continued as long as capacity remained; cessation would have been authorized by capacity-failure, not by preference for comfort. The continuation under increasing cost is the framework operating on its hardest case for the practitioner.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Epictetus continued teaching until shortly before his death (c. 135 CE). The school dissolved without successor. The Discourses' final books contain transcripts from this late period, in which the body's failure is explicitly acknowledged without doctrinal collapse — the empirical case promised earlier was supplied.

critical_incident_database.decision: Epictetus chose to refuse fee-differentials and individual-paid-counseling — keeping the school free of the market-pricing structure that would have allowed wealthy students to purchase preferential attention.
critical_incident_database.context: Throughout the Nicopolis decades, the imperial-period philosophical-teaching market had developed a tiered service structure: standard cohort lectures, premium small-group instruction, and individually-priced personal counseling. Epictetus' contemporaries operated in this market; the structure was the natural form of pedagogical commerce. Epictetus refused all of it: a single uniform cohort, no fee-tier differentiation, no paid individual sessions. The school operated on minimal voluntary support and student-housing independent of the lecture-attendance structure.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that fee-differentials would create a two-tier student body: a paying inner circle and a non-paying outer circle, with predictable corruption of the inner circle (the teacher's calibration to their preferences) and resentment in the outer. He noticed that paid individual counseling would convert teaching from public diagnostic into private service, which would change the teacher's standing relationship to the student. He noticed that the market-pricing structure was load-bearing for his contemporaries' reputations and was therefore resistance-inducing to discard.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the preservation of the cohort's homogeneity as diagnostic environment. A subordinate goal was refusing to convert philosophy into a tiered consumer service. A further goal was modeling, for students, the refusal of differential treatment by class — a refusal whose operational visibility mattered to the doctrine.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected the standard tiered fee structure of his contemporaries. He rejected accepting wealthy patrons whose support would have come with implicit access-expectations. He rejected paid individual counseling. He rejected designating any student as an inner-circle or honored-cohort member.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed pedagogical structure as an extension of doctrine: how the school operated transmitted doctrine more durably than what was lectured about. Differential treatment by wealth would have transmitted a different doctrine than the one he taught. Structural uniformity was not minor administrative housekeeping but operational expression of the framework.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected lower revenue, smaller scale, and continued financial precariousness. He expected the school to be unable to expand or institutionalize. He accepted these as compatible with the function. He did not expect — but achieved — that the structural choices would make the school's operations textually visible later as evidence of a non-standard pedagogical practice.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher of standard imperial practice would have implemented tiered fees, accepted wealthy patrons, offered paid individual counseling, and used the differential-revenue to expand the school's operations and physical infrastructure. The school would have institutionalized; the doctrine's operational character would have been corrupted by the differential-treatment structure.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his framework treated structural choices as primary transmission-mechanism. The market-pricing structure would have transmitted, alongside the lectures, a value-architecture incompatible with the doctrine. The refusal is therefore not an aesthetic choice but an operational requirement of the doctrine he taught.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The Nicopolis school remained materially modest throughout his lifetime. No tier structure ever developed. The cohort retained the diagnostic homogeneity that Arrian's transcripts preserve. The structural choice limited the school's institutional durability — it dissolved on Epictetus' death — but produced the textual conditions under which the recorded teaching could survive in identifiable form.

critical_incident_database.decision: When students arrived from distinct social classes — slaves and freedmen alongside senators and equestrians — Epictetus chose to deliver identical lectures with identical diagnostic register to all, refusing to soften critique for high-status students or sharpen it for low-status ones.
critical_incident_database.context: The Nicopolis cohort was unusually mixed by ancient standards: alongside the Roman senatorial-class students who were the school's most visible alumni, the lectures were attended by freedmen, traveling artisans, and (per scattered Discourses references) at least one identifiable slave. The standard imperial-philosophical practice was to operate either as a senatorial-philosopher (audience: equestrian and senatorial) or as a popular-philosopher (audience: ordinary citizens), with curriculum and register calibrated to one or the other. Epictetus operated as both at once.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that any visible register-shift by audience class would have communicated, alongside the doctrine, that class-membership was a categorically relevant variable for philosophical engagement — undermining the prohairesis-doctrine's foundational claim that the relevant capacities were universally human. He noticed that mixed cohorts created mutual diagnostic exposure: the senator who heard the slave being addressed identically learned something about the senator's own standing relative to philosophy.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was operational consistency of the framework across class. A subordinate goal was the cohort's mutual recognition as fellow practitioners. A further goal was demonstrating, to himself and to students, that his own slave-origin had not been a transient biographical fact but a continuing operational anchor.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected class-segmented instruction. He rejected the senatorial-philosopher pattern. He rejected the popular-philosopher pattern. He rejected the option of identical content with class-calibrated register, which would have been a smaller version of the same compromise.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed class as an external on the same logical level as wealth, body, and reputation — relevant to the diagnostic content (different students would have different specific corruptions) but irrelevant to the diagnostic register. Identical register was the operational signature of the framework.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected occasional friction with high-status students who had not previously been addressed identically with their inferiors. He expected the friction to function as additional diagnostic material. He expected the cohort's class-mixed character to be one of the school's distinguishing features, which it became.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher of standard practice would have segmented by class, would have produced a senatorial-courtier register and a popular register, and would have lost the operational signature that identical-register transmitted. The school's class-mixed cohort would have dissolved into class-stratified attention.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because the prohairesis-doctrine made class-irrelevance not a value-claim but a structural feature of the framework. Class-segmented instruction would have been operationally inconsistent with the doctrine being taught. His own slave-origin made the inconsistency vivid in a way no born-free teacher could have matched.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The Nicopolis school's cohort retained its class-mixed character throughout his lifetime. The Discourses' surviving texts preserve named students from across the social range, and the register is identifiably uniform. The structural choice produced the unusual textual feature that modern readers can identify Epictetus' lectures as addressed to no specific class — a feature directly traceable to the operational decision he made repeatedly across decades.

critical_incident_database.decision: Epictetus chose to use his own slave-origin as continuing pedagogical material — referencing his time under Epaphroditus, the broken-leg incident, and the experience of slavery itself within his lectures — rather than concealing the biography or treating it as a transcended past.
critical_incident_database.context: The Nicopolis-period Discourses contain numerous references to Epictetus' time as a slave: the master's shoemaker, the broken leg, the experience of being subject to another's will, and (in Discourses 4.1) an extended dialogue on slavery and freedom that draws on his lived experience. The conventional Roman pattern for a freedman of philosophical accomplishment was to minimize the slave-past as completed transition. Epictetus reversed this: the slave-experience became permanent operational vocabulary.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that his slave-experience was unique pedagogical capital — the doctrine's claim that 'no one is more enslaved than the courtier of Caesar' had vastly more force coming from a former slave than from a born-free philosopher. He noticed that minimizing the slave-past would forfeit the capital. He noticed that the standard pattern of minimization was itself an example of the framework's diagnosed corruption: treating an external (former-slave status) as identity-relevant, which the doctrine instructed against.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was deploying the slave-experience as load-bearing pedagogical material. A subordinate goal was modeling, in his own self-references, the practice he was teaching: an external (slave-origin) was data, not identity. A further goal was the demonstration that the doctrine had been viable even under the conditions of slavery itself — the empirical case the doctrine most needed.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected the conventional minimization of slave-origin. He rejected the alternative extreme of organizing his self-presentation around slave-origin (as a counter-cultural identity), which would have inverted the same category-error. He rejected referring to the experience only in confessional registers. He rejected the option of structuring the references as evidence of his exceptional resilience, which would have substituted personal pride for philosophical demonstration.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the slave-experience as data-source: the lectures could draw on it the way a doctor draws on an illness he himself had recovered from. The framing was structurally identical to incident-012 (the lameness as pedagogical material): use the empirical case the body and biography provide.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the references to function as load-bearing demonstration alongside the lameness-references. He expected modern-Roman students to find the framing initially uncomfortable (a freedman addressing senators as fellow students of identical standing) and to absorb the discomfort as part of the diagnostic. He expected the references to enter Arrian's transcripts (which they did).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher of standard imperial practice would have minimized the slave-past as completed biographical transition. The doctrine would have been articulated; the empirical demonstration that it had been viable under slave-conditions would have been forfeit. The lectures' force, especially on the freedom-and-slavery topic, would have been substantially reduced.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his framework's class-irrelevance feature (incident-022) and externals-as-data feature (incident-005, 012) jointly required treating slave-experience as ordinary pedagogical material. The conventional minimization would have been a category-error within his own framework. The continuing references are the framework operating cleanly on the most personal element of his biography.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The Discourses' references to slave-experience became one of the most-cited features of the text in subsequent reception, particularly in the modern period. Discourses 4.1 became the canonical Stoic text on freedom, drawing its force from the author's lived experience of unfreedom. The pattern produced the textual feature that no other major Stoic text shares: a senior philosopher routinely referencing his own slave past as transferable empirical material.

critical_incident_database.decision: When a man came to Epictetus distraught about his sick daughter — having fled the bedside because he could not bear to watch her suffering — Epictetus chose not to console him but to demonstrate that the man's flight was a category error: the man had not loved his daughter more than himself, he had loved his own affective state more than his daughter.
critical_incident_database.context: Recorded as Discourses 1.11. The man was apparently of some standing (he came to Epictetus rather than the household physician, suggesting philosophical access). His daughter was ill at home. He had left her bedside because, by his own account, he could not bear the sight of her suffering. He arrived seeking sympathy framed as confirmation that his flight reflected the depth of his love. Epictetus refused the framing and reconstructed the man's behavior as the opposite of what the man had described.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the man's framing — 'I love her too much to watch' — was an inverted description of the act, since love-of-her would have produced presence-with-her, while love-of-his-own-affective-state produced flight-from-her. He noticed that consolation would have ratified the inversion and left the man with both the inverted self-description and the operational pattern of leaving his daughter alone in suffering.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was correcting the inverted self-description so that the man could re-evaluate the flight and, on the corrected evaluation, return to his daughter. A subordinate goal was the cohort's witness of the structural test for self-descriptions of love.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected sympathetic consolation. He rejected silent listening. He rejected dismissal. He rejected the option of philosophical instruction in the abstract that did not address the man's specific case.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the man's flight as a diagnostic instance of the love-displacement pattern: confusing love-of-the-other with love-of-one's-own-affective-state-around-the-other. The framing was structurally identical to incident-011 (the adulterer's self-description), with the same reversal-test deployed.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the man to either reject the diagnostic and leave (preserving the inversion) or to accept it and return to the daughter. He expected the cohort to absorb the love-displacement diagnostic.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher in the consolatory register would have absorbed the man's framing and confirmed it: yes, you love her so much that you cannot bear to watch. The man would have left consoled and his daughter would have remained alone in her suffering. The framework would have functioned as anti-philosophy.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his pattern-recognition of the love-displacement category was reflexive by this stage of his career (the same diagnostic underlies incident-011). The willingness to apply it under conditions of acute distress — when consolation was the conventional response — is the framework operating on its hardest live case for the teacher's social-comfort.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Discourses 1.11 became one of the canonical Stoic texts on family attachment and ordinary love. The man's identity and subsequent behavior are not preserved. The diagnostic structure — that fled-from suffering reveals love-of-own-affective-state, not love-of-other — entered the cohort's repertoire and is the structural ancestor of much subsequent Stoic and Christian thinking on attachment.

critical_incident_database.decision: When a rhetorician traveling to Rome to argue a high-stakes lawsuit stopped at Nicopolis to seek philosophical advice, Epictetus chose to refuse advice on the lawsuit and instead diagnosed the rhetorician's entire calibration of his life around a contest whose outcome was outside his control.
critical_incident_database.context: Recorded as Discourses 3.9. The rhetorician arrived nervous, ambitious, and seeking some philosophical equipment that would help him in the upcoming case. The conventional response from a philosopher of his class would have been technical advice on speech-craft, ethical advice on advocacy, or psychological advice on managing courtroom anxiety. Epictetus delivered none of these. He instead asked the rhetorician what he hoped the philosophical contact would do for him, and reconstructed the request itself as evidence of the underlying value-displacement.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the rhetorician was seeking a tool, not transformation — the request was for instrumental philosophical assistance with a goal whose evaluation as goal had not been examined. He noticed that delivering the tool would have served the rhetorician's confused goal-architecture; refusing the tool and diagnosing the architecture was the harder but appropriate response. He noticed that the cohort would observe whether he calibrated to instrumental philosophical-service requests.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was refusing the conversion of the school into instrumental philosophical-service. A subordinate goal was the rhetorician's diagnostic. A further goal was the cohort's witness of how a high-status visitor with a felt urgent need was treated.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected the technical advice the rhetorician wanted. He rejected the consoling psychological advice (manage your nerves) that would have provided functional help. He rejected the dismissal of the request without engagement. He rejected the option of providing some philosophical content while preserving the instrumental frame.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the lawsuit as paradigmatic external — outcome dependent on factors beyond the rhetorician's prohairesis — and the rhetorician's calibration around it as the textbook category-error of treating an external as up-to-us. The framing reduced the rhetorician's specific case to an instance of the doctrine's most basic distinction.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the rhetorician to be unsettled (the diagnostic would land), to perhaps reject the diagnostic and leave, and to perhaps return later if the lawsuit went badly. He expected the cohort to observe the structural pattern — request for tool, refusal of tool, diagnostic of underlying request — and to absorb it as transferable.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher operating in the standard advisor-philosopher register would have provided some combination of technical, ethical, and psychological assistance, would have left the rhetorician's goal-architecture unexamined, and would have functioned as instrumental support for the very value-displacement the doctrine claimed to address. The school would have operated as an upmarket advisory service.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his framework treated requests-for-tools as themselves diagnostic data: what the visitor wanted from philosophy revealed what the visitor thought philosophy was for. Honoring the request would have ratified the misconception. The structural pattern — refuse the requested service, diagnose the request — is identifiable across the Discourses and is one of the framework's signature operational moves.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Discourses 3.9 became one of the texts demonstrating the school's refusal to operate as instrumental advisory. The rhetorician's identity and the lawsuit's outcome are not preserved. The pattern entered the cohort's transferable inventory: when a visitor arrived asking for a tool, the diagnostic moved up one level to the request itself.

critical_incident_database.decision: When the emperor Hadrian visited Nicopolis (or otherwise developed the 'familiar' relationship the Historia Augusta records), Epictetus chose to maintain the identical diagnostic register he used with humble students — neither courting the imperial connection nor performing visible refusal of it.
critical_incident_database.context: The Historia Augusta records that Hadrian was 'on familiar terms' (familiariter usus est) with Epictetus. The exact form (visit, correspondence, sustained relationship) is not specified; the source is late and partly suspect, but consistent with Hadrian's eastern travels and his documented interest in philosophy. Whatever the form, Epictetus' response is identifiable from its absence: no transcript records a different register for the emperor, no collected letters survive (or were preserved as significant), no shift in the school's operations followed.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the imperial connection was the maximum-stakes test of the register-stability principle he had operated for forty years. Any visible accommodation toward the emperor would have collapsed the school's standing demonstration that the diagnostic did not bend to power. Any visible refusal would have substituted theatrical principle-display for the operational consistency the framework required.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the preservation of operational consistency under the maximum-visibility test-case. A subordinate goal was preventing the imperial connection from converting the school into an imperial-philosophical institution.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected courting the connection. He rejected refusing it visibly (theatrics). He rejected adjusting register marginally. He rejected the option of producing a public document on the connection (a treatise dedicated to Hadrian, a letter to the emperor preserved in copies) which would have converted the connection into reputation-capital.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the imperial connection as a special instance of an ordinary case: a visitor of unusually high social standing, to be treated identically with all other visitors. The framing was the operational implementation of incident-022's class-irrelevance principle, applied to its hardest case.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the connection to be invisible to the school's daily operations and to leave no significant textual trace, both of which appear to have occurred. He did not expect — and would not have welcomed — the connection becoming a defining feature of his historical reputation.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A philosopher of standard imperial practice would have used the connection — letters, visits, dedications, courtly proximity — to elevate the school into an imperial institution. The conventional pathway would have produced institutional permanence and substantial reputation-capital. The framework would have transmitted, alongside the doctrine, the operational lesson that imperial connection was an asset to be deployed.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because the imperial connection was, in his framework, the textbook external whose deployment for institutional benefit would have demonstrated exactly the corruption his doctrine identified. Refusing to deploy it required maintaining identical register through the visit or correspondence, which required the prior decades of operational consistency. The non-deployment is the framework operating on the test it was always headed toward.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The Hadrian connection left almost no preserved textual trace — exactly the outcome the framework predicted. The school continued unchanged. The imperial association did not institutionalize the school, did not generate reputation-capital, and did not corrupt the cohort's class-mixed character. The non-event quality of the connection's outcome is itself the operational signature.

critical_incident_database.decision: Facing his own death by physical decline (c. 135 CE), Epictetus chose to continue applying the doctrine to his own dying — treating the body's failure as the textbook external case it had always been in the lectures, neither demanding philosophical theatrics from himself nor allowing the doctrine to weaken under the actual pressure.
critical_incident_database.context: Late ancient testimony on Epictetus' death is sparse: he died, apparently in Nicopolis, after the body's gradual failure that the late Discourses had been documenting. There is no surviving deathbed scene, no recorded last words of philosophical drama, no successor designation. The testimony of Lucian (peripheral) and the absence of dramatic accounts in the Stoic tradition (which preserved deathbed scenes for Seneca, Cato, Thrasea) is itself informative: there was no scene to preserve because the dying conformed to the daily-life pattern, with no special philosophical performance.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that the body's failure was the textbook external case the doctrine had always governed, and that any registration-of-the-dying as special would have admitted that the doctrine had not, in fact, governed it equivalently with all the prior externals. He noticed that deathbed-philosophical-theatrics had been a feature of the Stoic tradition (Cato, Seneca) and that the doctrine he had taught did not authorize them — they were performance, not application.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the doctrine's continued operation through the final case. A subordinate goal was the absence of dramatic performance — the operational signature of the framework was that the dying was ordinary, not theatrical.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected the Cato-Seneca model of philosophical-deathbed theatrics. He rejected designating successors and producing valedictory addresses. He rejected the alternative of detached resignation that would have substituted withdrawal for continued application. He rejected complaint, fear, or doctrinal compromise.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed his own dying as the final ordinary case. The body's failure was external; the prohairesis remained where it had always been. The framing did not generate special operations because the doctrine had never authorized them.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected the dying to leave little textual trace — exactly because the doctrine's correct application produced no special record. He expected the school to dissolve. He expected no posthumous institutionalization.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A philosopher of the Stoic tradition's dramatic-death subgenre would have produced a recorded scene: gathered students, philosophical last-words, demonstrative refusal of fear, perhaps a documented voluntary acceleration of the death. The dying would have entered the philosophical literature alongside Cato and Seneca. The framework would have transmitted, alongside the doctrine, the operational lesson that the death itself was an occasion for philosophical performance.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his framework's operational signature was non-theatricality. The dying could not be a special case because the doctrine had governed all externals identically; treating the death as special would have demonstrated that the framework had been performance, not architecture. The absence-of-scene at his death is the framework's clearest operational signature.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Almost no testimony of his death survives. The school dissolved. The doctrine survived through Arrian's transcripts. The non-deathbed quality of his ending is structurally consistent with every prior operational decision he made and is, paradoxically, one of the strongest pieces of evidence that the framework was lived rather than performed.

critical_incident_database.decision: When students confronted Epictetus with their own fears of death, he chose to refuse both consolatory reframing and dramatic affirmation — instead deploying his standard diagnostic on the fear itself, treating the fear as evidence of the student's continued mis-location of self in the body.
critical_incident_database.context: Discourses 2.1 (titled in surviving editions 'On the absence of fear and the fact that what we fear is itself the source of our being'), 3.10, and 4.1 record sustained engagement with the fear-of-death topic. Students arrived with the fear; the fear was not unusual. The conventional Stoic responses — both consolatory ('death is natural') and dramatic ('the philosopher does not fear') — were available. Epictetus delivered neither. He instead treated the fear as data and ran the prohairesis-diagnostic on the student's underlying valuation.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: He noticed that consolatory reframing and dramatic affirmation were both performance-responses that addressed the student's affective state without correcting the underlying mis-location. He noticed that the fear-of-death was diagnostic of the same mis-location every other fear was diagnostic of: the student had located self in the body, and the body's predicted termination produced fear. He noticed that addressing the fear without correcting the mis-location would produce, at most, a temporary affective adjustment.
cdm_probes.active_goals: His operative goal was the diagnostic correction of the mis-location, not the management of the affective state. A subordinate goal was modeling, for the cohort, the principle that all fears were structurally identical at the underlying-architecture level.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: He rejected consolatory reframing. He rejected dramatic affirmation. He rejected dismissal. He rejected the option of partial-diagnostic followed by partial-consolation, which would have hybridized the responses and corrupted both.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: He framed the fear as instance, not exception. The same diagnostic that had been deployed on fear-of-loss-of-property, fear-of-loss-of-reputation, and fear-of-illness was the correct diagnostic for fear-of-death. Differentiating death as a special category would have introduced an exemption that the framework did not authorize.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: He expected partial transformation in students who could absorb the diagnostic and persistent fear in those who could not. He expected the diagnostic itself to be transferable to all other fears, which was the durable curricular outcome.

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: A teacher operating in either consolatory or dramatic Stoic registers would have managed the affective state without addressing the underlying architecture, and would have produced students who felt-better-about-death without being-different-about-self-location. The school's operational distinction would have been forfeit on the most pedagogically demanded topic.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: Epictetus diverged because his framework's structural feature was that all fears reduced to the same diagnostic. Treating death as special would have introduced a category-exemption the framework did not authorize and would have demonstrated that the framework was, at the limit, conventional. The willingness to apply the standard diagnostic on the maximally-loaded case is the framework operating on its hardest test for the doctrine's reductionism.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The relevant Discourses became Stoicism's most-cited material on death. The pattern — diagnose fear-of-death as instance of mis-location-of-self, not as special category — entered the Stoic tradition as the structural alternative to consolatory and dramatic responses. Students who absorbed the diagnostic acquired a transferable instrument that operated on all fears identically, which was the curricular goal.
